# --output-jsonv2 opts into encoding/json/v2 MarshalWrite for signal marshaling.
# This is generation-only (no go test / muxt check) because building the output
# requires a toolchain with GOEXPERIMENT=jsonv2; the assertions confirm the
# standard library encoding/json/v2 is used and the go-json-experiment backport
# is never referenced.
muxt generate --use-receiver-type=Server --use-datastar --output-jsonv2

grep 'json "encoding/json/v2"' template_routes.go
grep 'json.MarshalWrite' template_routes.go
! grep 'go-json-experiment' template_routes.go
! grep 'json.Marshal\(' template_routes.go

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "POST /count datastar(marshalJSON(Increment(ctx)))" -}}{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.25
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"context"
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

type Count struct {
	Count int `json:"count"`
}

func (Server) Increment(ctx context.Context) Count {
	return Count{Count: 5}
}
